candidates.description: ♾️ Private Agent Fleet with Spec Coding. Each agent gets their own desktop. Run Claude, Codex, Gemini and open models on a full private GenAI Stack ♾️
An open-source AI agent that brings the power of Gemini directly into your terminal.

readme.text:
# qbit ## Open Source Agentic Terminal Application [![macOS](https://img.shields.io/badge/macOS-000000?style=flat&logo=apple&logoColor=white)](#quickstart) [![Rust](https://img.shields.io/badge/Rust-000000?style=flat&logo=rust&logoColor=white)](https://www.rust-lang.org/) [![Tauri](https://img.shields.io/badge/Tauri_2-24C8D8?style=flat&logo=tauri&logoColor=white)](https://tauri.app/) [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE) ![Last Commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/qbit-ai/qbit) [![PRs Welcome](https://img.shields.io/badge/PRs-welcome-brightgreen.svg)](https://github.com/qbit-ai/qbit/pulls) [Quickstart](#quickstart) • [Docs](docs/README.md) • [Development](docs/development.md)
--- ## About Qbit - Free and open-source. - No account or subscription required. Bring your own keys. - Fully transparent. No mysteries, no bullshit. - Empowers users with information and full control. --- ## Features
### Project Management Organize workspaces with project shortcuts and quick access [![Home](docs/img/home.png)](docs/img/home.png) ### Unified Timeline Seamless conversation with AI, tool results, and terminal output [![Timeline](docs/img/timeline.png)](docs/img/timeline.png) ### Model Selection Choose from multiple AI providers and models [![Model Selection](docs/img/model-selection.png)](docs/img/model-selection.png) ### Inline Text Editing Review and edit AI-generated content before applying [![Text Editor](docs/img/text-editor.png)](docs/img/text-editor.png) ### Tool Transparency Full visibility into every tool call and its execution [![Tool Details](docs/img/tool-details.png)](docs/img/tool-details.png) ### Sub-agent Execution Detailed view of sub-agent tasks and their results [![Sub-agent Details](docs/img/sub-agent-details.png)](docs/img/sub-agent-details.png) ### Git Integration Built-in version control with diff visualization [![Git Integration](docs/img/git-integration.png)](docs/img/git-integration.png)
--- ## Quickstart ### Install (macOS) ```bash brew tap qbit-ai/tap brew install --cask qbit ``` ### Run from source ```bash git clone https://github.com/qbit-ai/qbit.git cd qbit just install just dev ``` --- ## Linux Installation ### Install from release build Download and extract the release build: ```bash curl -L -o qbit_x64.app.tar.gz \ https://github.com/qbit-ai/qbit/releases/download/v0.2.13/qbit_x64.app.tar.gz mkdir -p qbit-release tar -xzf qbit_x64.app.tar.gz -C qbit-release ``` Add the binary to your `PATH` (adjust as needed for your system): ```bash sudo install -m 755 qbit-release/qbit /usr/local/bin/qbit ``` For source builds and Linux prerequisites, see [Getting started](docs/getting-started.md). --- ## Documentation Start here: - [Docs index](docs/README.md) - [Getting started](docs/getting-started.md) - [Configuration](docs/configuration.md) - [Providers](docs/providers.md) Using Qbit: - [Workspaces](docs/workspaces.md) - [Agent modes](docs/agent-modes.md) - [Agent skills](docs/agent-skills.md) - [Tool use](docs/tool-use.md) - [MCP integration](docs/mcp.md) Developing: - [Development](docs/development.md) - [Architecture](docs/architecture.md) Evaluation / benchmarks: - [Rig evals](docs/rig-evals.md) - [SWE-bench](docs/swebench.md)

items.name: v0.2.42
items.body_excerpt:
## [0.2.42](https://github.com/qbit-ai/qbit/compare/v0.2.41...v0.2.42) (2026-03-06)


### Features

* **models:** add GPT-5.4 model with reasoning effort variants ([7570671](https://github.com/qbit-ai/qbit/commit/757067193bacb5f2dd59c7f67f85b55b09ea89c8))
* **models:** add GPT-5.4 model with reasoning effort variants ([3a038d4](https://github.com/qbit-ai/qbit/commit/3a038d48fdb7cd4a440005322d416c9ae3f0b90c))


### Bug Fixes

* **ai:** preserve partial output and add resilient stream-start retries ([ba7688d](https://github.com/qbit-ai/qbit/commit/ba7688dbbea332e1a464913ab71f28c5298e64c0))
* **ai:** preserve partial output and harden stream-start retries ([32603a8](https://github.com/qbit-ai/qbit/commit/32603a8538595169d8b06899e8b86ce028cba0dd))
* resolve lint, formatting, and flaky test issues ([48a632e](https://github.com/qbit-ai/qbit/commit/48a632ed5bbdc6e2222a805fc0f48c1533ee69e2))
* **tab-bar:** hide pending command indicator during fullterm sessions ([2390813](https://github.com/qbit-ai/qbit/commit/239081381f573f1a920d144808da272367cc10f0))
* **terminal:** suppress fullterm output from timeline ([96f24eb](https://github.com/qbit-ai/qbit/commit/96f24ebd794e40b5b7d7251ab7c32284a55f1eef))
* **terminal:** suppress fullterm output from timeline ([329ec4b](https://github.com/qbit-ai/qbit/commit/329ec4bfda92d22a9c78d5f2ba07fd08cdfe3fba))

# qbit ## Open Source Agentic Terminal Application [![macOS](https://img.shields.io/badge/macOS-000000?style=flat&logo=apple&logoColor=white)](#quickstart) [![Rust](https://img.shields.io/badge/Rust-000000?style=flat&logo=rust&logoColor=white)](https://www.rust-lang.org/) [![Tauri](https://img.shields.io/badge/Tauri_2-24C8D8?style=flat&logo=tauri&logoColor=white)](https://tauri.app/) [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE) ![Last Commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/qbit-ai/qbit) [![PRs Welcome](https://img.shields.io/badge/PRs-welcome-brightgreen.svg)](https://github.com/qbit-ai/qbit/pulls) [Quickstart](#quickstart) • [Docs](docs/README.md) • [Development](docs/development.md)
--- ## About Qbit - Free and open-source. - No account or subscription required. Bring your own keys. - Fully transparent. No mysteries, no bullshit. - Empowers users with information and full control. --- ## Features
### Project Management Organize workspaces with project shortcuts and quick access [![Home](docs/img/home.png)](docs/img/home.png) ### Unified Timeline Seamless conversation with AI, tool results, and terminal output [![Timeline](docs/img/timeline.png)](docs/img/timeline.png) ### Model Selection Choose from multiple AI providers and models [![Model Selection](docs/img/model-selection.png)](docs/img/model-selection.png) ### Inline Text Editing Review and edit AI-generated content before applying [![Text Editor](docs/img/text-editor.png)](docs/img/text-editor.png) ### Tool Transparency Full visibility into every tool call and its execution [![Tool Details](docs/img/tool-details.png)](docs/img/tool-details.png) ### Sub-agent Execution Detailed view of sub-agent tasks and their results [![Sub-agent Details](docs/img/sub-agent-details.png)](docs/img/sub-agent-details.png) ### Git Integration Built-in version control with diff visualization [![Git Integration](docs/img/git-integration.png)](docs/img/git-integration.png)
--- ## Quickstart ### Install (macOS) ```bash brew tap qbit-ai/tap brew install --cask qbit ``` ### Run from source ```bash git clone https://github.com/qbit-ai/qbit.git cd qbit just install just dev ``` --- ## Linux Installation ### Install from release build Download and extract the release build: ```bash curl -L -o qbit_x64.app.tar.gz \ https://github.com/qbit-ai/qbit/releases/download/v0.2.13/qbit_x64.app.tar.gz mkdir -p qbit-release tar -xzf qbit_x64.app.tar.gz -C qbit-release ``` Add the binary to your `PATH` (adjust as needed for your system): ```bash sudo install -m 755 qbit-release/qbit /usr/local/bin/qbit ``` For source builds and Linux prerequisites, see [Getting started](docs/getting-started.md). --- ## Documentation Start here: - [Docs index](docs/README.md) - [Getting started](docs/getting-started.md) - [Configuration](docs/configuration.md) - [Providers](docs/providers.md) Using Qbit: - [Workspaces](docs/workspaces.md) - [Agent modes](docs/agent-modes.md) - [Agent skills](docs/agent-skills.md) - [Tool use](docs/tool-use.md) - [MCP integration](docs/mcp.md) Developing: - [Development](docs/development.md) - [Architecture](docs/architecture.md) Evaluation / benchmarks: - [Rig evals](docs/rig-evals.md) - [SWE-bench](docs/swebench.md)

hints: Inspect report artifact and llm_audits for details

scout.summary: qbit is an open-source, agentic terminal application built with Rust and Tauri, targeting macOS. It emphasizes user control, transparency, and local execution with support for custom AI models and endpoints. Recent releases show active development focused on project management, model integration (including GPT-5.4), and stream resilience. However, a known open issue indicates problems with custom OpenAI endpoint functionality.
highlights: Built with Rust and Tauri, targeting macOS with a native desktop experience.
Promotes user sovereignty: no account or subscription required; users bring their own API keys.
Features include project management, unified AI/terminal timeline, Git integration, and sub-agent workflows.
Recent releases added GPT-5.4 support and improved streaming reliability.
Repository includes comprehensive docs, testing (Playwright, Vitest), and modern tooling (Biome, pnpm).
risks: Open issue (ID:E-IS-001) reports that custom OpenAI endpoints are not working, potentially limiting flexibility for users relying on non-default providers.
Very recent release dates (e.g., v0.2.43 dated 2026-03-13) suggest possible timestamp anomalies or future-dated commits, which may indicate CI/release process irregularities.

inventor.summary: qbit is an agentic terminal application that integrates AI workflows directly into the developer's local environment, combining project management, model orchestration, and terminal interaction in a unified interface. It enables users to run AI agents with full control over models and keys, supporting advanced features like sub-agents, Git integration, and timeline-based conversation history.
highlights: Invents a novel fusion of terminal emulator and AI agent platform, allowing seamless interleaving of command-line output, tool results, and AI dialogue in a unified timeline (E-RD-005).
Introduces user-controlled project workspaces with shortcuts and recent directory management, enhancing developer workflow organization (E-RD-004, E-RL-001).
Supports customizable AI backends including GPT-5.4 with reasoning effort variants, enabling fine-grained control over model behavior (E-RL-002).
Employs a Tauri + Rust + React stack to deliver a native macOS desktop experience while maintaining open-source transparency and offline-first principles (E-RD-001, E-RD-003).
Visualizes complex agent interactions through dedicated UI components like model selection panels, sub-agent detail views, and Git-aware interfaces (E-RD-006, E-RD-007, E-RD-008).
risks: The core value proposition of 'bring your own key' is undermined by an unresolved bug preventing custom OpenAI endpoints from functioning (E-IS-001), limiting interoperability with alternative or self-hosted LLM providers.

Heavy reliance on cutting-edge, possibly speculative model versions (e.g., GPT-5.4) may introduce instability or compatibility issues if those models are not widely available or standardized (E-RL-002).

engineer.summary: qbit is a Rust-based agentic terminal application built with Tauri and React, targeting macOS with a focus on local, user-controlled AI workflows. It integrates terminal output, AI conversations, and tool results into a unified timeline, supports project management features, and enables custom model usage—including experimental models like GPT-5.4. The codebase shows active development with modern tooling (Biome, pnpm, Playwright), but suffers from a critical unresolved bug affecting custom OpenAI endpoint functionality.
highlights: Built on a performant Tauri + Rust backend with a React frontend, optimized for macOS and packaged as a native desktop app (E-RD-001).
Implements a unified timeline that interleaves AI responses, terminal output, and tool execution for cohesive developer workflows (E-RD-005).
Recent releases include robust streaming retries and partial output preservation to improve AI interaction reliability (E-RL-002).
Project management features allow users to organize workspaces, remove recent directories, and delete projects (E-RD-004, E-RL-001).
Comprehensive testing and linting infrastructure using Playwright, Vitest, and Biome indicates engineering discipline (E-RF-001).
risks: A known open issue (E-IS-001) breaks custom OpenAI endpoint support, directly contradicting the project’s 'bring your own key' promise and limiting provider flexibility.
Use of unreleased or speculative model identifiers like 'GPT-5.4' (E-RL-002) may cause runtime errors or confusion if those models are not available via standard APIs.
Future-dated release tags (e.g., v0.2.43 in March 2026) suggest potential CI/release automation misconfigurations that could affect version integrity.

skeptic.summary: While qbit presents itself as a transparent, user-controlled agentic terminal with cutting-edge AI integration, several red flags undermine its credibility: a critical bug breaks its core 'bring your own key' promise, speculative model support (e.g., GPT-5.4) appears premature, and anomalously future-dated releases suggest potential process or timestamp integrity issues.
highlights: Claims of full user control and 'no bullshit' transparency are contradicted by an unresolved bug preventing custom OpenAI endpoint usage (E-IS-001).
Marketing of GPT-5.4 support (E-RL-002) is highly suspect, as this model does not exist publicly as of 2024—raising concerns about vaporware or misleading feature claims.
Release versions dated in 2026 (E-RL-001, E-RL-002, E-RL-003) indicate either CI misconfiguration or fabricated timelines, eroding trust in release stability and versioning.
risks: Users cannot actually use alternative or self-hosted LLM endpoints due to the open bug (E-IS-001), directly violating the project’s foundational promise of sovereignty.
Reliance on non-existent or unreleased models like GPT-5.4 may render features unusable or create false expectations, damaging user trust and adoption.
Future-dated releases suggest poor release hygiene or automated pipeline errors, which could mask unstable builds or complicate debugging and reproducibility.
